title: AssetClassPlan
description: A class-family maintenance plan: one document holding an asset CLASS's base plan (versioned, like a job plan) plus the MODEL and INSTANCE override layers that inherit from it. The class defines the shared procedure once; models and instances derive from it at read time, taking local ownership only where they override, add or exclude. This extends the single-plan job-plan.schema.json — the class's versioned content mirrors a JobPlan version — and folds the version lifecycle back on top of Class -> Model -> Instance inheritance. One JSON document = one class family (all its models, instances and versions).

code.description: Human-facing unique class code, e.g. GEN, UPS, CHILLER.

name.description: Asset class name, e.g. Standby Generator.

workOrderTypeCode.description: The kind of work this plan drops, referencing a WorkOrderType by code (see work-order-type.schema.json). Work orders generated from this family take this type. Null defaults to PM (preventative).

rev.description: Optimistic-concurrency revision; bumped on every save.

activeVersion.description: Version number of the class base plan currently in the 'active' state, if any. At most one version is active. Model/instance overrides layer on top of the resolved active version.

versions.description: All versions of the CLASS base plan, retained forever. A version is immutable once it leaves 'draft'. This is the shared procedure that models and instances inherit from and override.

models.description: Model-level override layers within this class (e.g. Caterpillar 3512 under Standby Generator). Each is a delta over the class's active version, and each carries its own instances. Overrides are current-state, not versioned — the work-order snapshot pins the class version and captures the resolved model/instance result.

classVersion.description: One version of the class base plan. Mirrors job-plan.schema.json#/$defs/version, adding attribute-level 'enforced' locks so the class can forbid descendants from overriding safety-critical items.

lifecycleState.description: draft -> approved -> active -> superseded -> retired. Only 'draft' is editable.

attrs.description: Class-level plan attributes, each lockable. A locked (enforced) attribute cannot be overridden by any model or instance.

frequencies.description: Work-order generation frequencies the class plan can drop at (task x frequency matrix; each task maps via task.frequencyIds).

tasks.description: The class's ordered task list. Each task may be enforced (descendants cannot exclude or re-map it).

parameters.description: Named values the class plan is parameterised on. Each may be enforced (locked at the class value) or left open for models/instances to override.

value.description: The class default for this attribute.

enforced.description: When true, the class locks this value; no model or instance may override it. Enforcing clears any existing overrides below.

task.description: A class task. Extends job-plan.schema.json#/$defs/task (which keys on 'seq') with a stable 'id' — overrides and exclusions reference tasks by id, not position — and an 'enforced' lock.

id.description: Stable task id, referenced by model/instance exclusions and mapOverrides.

responseType.description: Expected response captured when the task is performed.

unit.description: Unit for a numeric reading, e.g. V, A, mOhm.

acceptableRange.description: [low, high] acceptance bounds for a numeric reading.

frequencyIds.description: Ids of the frequencies this task drops at (its ticked cells in the matrix). A task may belong to several frequencies.

enforced.description: When true, the class locks this task: descendants cannot exclude it or re-map its frequencies.

parameter.description: A class parameter. Extends job-plan.schema.json#/$defs/parameter with a stable 'id' (override keys reference it) and an 'enforced' lock.

id.description: Stable parameter id, referenced by model/instance paramOverrides.

enforced.description: When true, the class locks this parameter at defaultValue; no model or instance may override it.

modelNode.description: A model-level override layer (e.g. Caterpillar 3512). Holds only its deltas over the resolved class version (see $defs/overrideLayer for the field semantics), plus its instances.

instanceNode.description: An instance-level override layer (a physical unit, e.g. GEN-1 @ SYD1). Same deltas as a model (see $defs/overrideLayer), plus its site; instances have no children.

site.description: Site code where this instance lives, e.g. SYD1.

overrideLayer.$comment: Not used directly as a node type; it is the canonical definition of the delta fields a model or instance may apply over its parent. modelNode and instanceNode reference these property schemas by pointer so the semantics are declared once. Empty layer = pure inheritance. An override is frozen against parent change until reverted; an enforced parent field cannot be overridden.

attrOverrides.description: Attribute value overrides, keyed by attribute name (e.g. estimatedLabourHours). Only unlocked class attributes may appear.

paramOverrides.description: Parameter value overrides, keyed by class parameter id. Only unlocked class parameters may appear.

addedFreqs.description: Frequencies added at this level, on top of the inherited matrix columns.

addedTasks.description: Tasks added at this level, beyond the inherited class tasks.

exclusions.description: Ids of inherited (non-enforced) class tasks this level removes.

mapOverrides.description: Per-task frequency re-mapping, keyed by inherited task id -> array of frequency ids. Replaces that task's inherited ticked cells at this level. Enforced tasks cannot be re-mapped.
